title: Summary suit procedure - faster recovery for liquidated claims
p1:
Most people assume recovering a debt means years in court.
But civil law already has a shortcut most professionals never hear about
p2: Misconception: every unpaid invoice needs full trial. Reality: Summary suit under Order 37 CPC lets certain money claims move fast if based on written contract, invoice, negotiable instrument. surprise insight: defendant cannot automatically defend; must first seek permission ("leave to defend") and show valid reason. many don't know this gatekeeping
p3: If claim is liquidated (fixed amount) like unpaid invoices, loans, or promissory notes, you can file summary suit. court may directly pass decree if defendant fails to justify defence. practical takeaway: businesses should structure transactions in writing; it dramatically increases recovery speed when disputes arise. Even courts treat these cases differently because evidence is already in writing. That is why recovery is often much faster compared to ordinary civil suits
conclusion: Have you seen cases where invoices remained unpaid for years simply because parties didn't use summary suit route?
